title: Audi Bets on Split Branding To Regain Spark for China Car Buyers
summary: Audi AG is doubling down on its dual-marque strategy in China, sticking to its goal of delivering more than 600,000 vehicles this year as it navigates a market where it once enjoyed institutional cachet.

title: Oil Eases After Pakistan Says US-Iran Peace Talks Are Expected
summary: Oil eased as officials in Pakistan said a second round of US-Iran talks was expected, without saying when the negotiations would take place or at what level.

title: SNB Chief Economist Lenz to Retire, With Brown Set to Take Over
summary: Swiss National Bank Chief Economist Carlos Lenz is set to retire at the end of the year and will be succeeded by Martin Brown, the current director of the central bank’s academic research institution.

title: Stocks Rise on Iran Hopes as Powell Probe to End: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks rose on optimism the US and Iran are moving toward talks after days of deadlock, fueling hopes for a diplomatic way out of the war that has clouded the economic outlook. Bond yields fell as a probe of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell is being dropped.

title: One by One, Europe’s Safest Government Bonds Fall From Grace
summary: The traditional hierarchy of debt in Europe is facing its latest shake-up as worsening public finances in Belgium threaten to turn some of the region’s once-safest bonds into a risky bet.

title: DOJ Expected to Drop Powell Investigation: ABC
summary: US Attorney Jeanine Pirro said in a social media post that she plans to drop her controversial investigation into building-renovation cost overruns by the Federal Reserve. Mike McKee reports on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Finma Says Immediate Mythos Access Would Pose Systemic Bank Risk
summary: Switzerland’s top financial regulator said giving banks quick and easy access to Anthropic PBC’s artificial intelligence tool Mythos would create a severe risk for the country’s financial system.

title: Inflation Fears Drive Consumer Sentiment to Record Low
summary: The University of Michigan’s final April sentiment index dropped to 49.8 this month from 53.3 in March. While that was slightly improved from the preliminary reading, it remained the lowest in data back to 1978. Mike McKee reports on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Why Our Labor Market Is Forever Changed
summary: The US is not in a recession. So why is the labor market so weak? Bloomberg Opinion columnist Kathryn Anne Edwards says employer concentration is to blame. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Earnings Are Surprisingly Strong, Says Morgan Stanley's Wilson
summary: Morgan Stanley Chief US Equity Strategist and CIO Mike Wilson says this earnings recovery is stronger than many people think. He speaks on Bloomberg Open Interest. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: French Power Slumps to Lowest Since 2013 as Solar Output Booms
summary: European power prices plunged to multiyear lows as solar generation surged, flooding the region’s grids.

title: Stocks Can Handle 'Stalemate Situation' in Iran, Yardeni Says
summary: Ed Yardeni, president of Yardeni Research, says markets can withstand $100 a barrel oil prices on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hedge Fund Launched by Instacart Co-Founder Has AI Call Shots
summary: Instacart co-founder Apoorva Mehta launched a hedge fund that relies on an army of artificial intelligence agents, one of the few using that technology to essentially replace fundamental portfolio managers.

title: Nasdaq 100 Jumps as Intel’s Blockbuster Sales View Impresses
summary: The Nasdaq 100 Index jumped on Friday as a blockbuster sales forecast from Intel Corp. fueled a rally in technology stocks.

title: Saudi Arabia’s Hormuz Oil Bypass Is Yet to Maintain Full Exports
summary: Saudi Arabia has achieved a huge boost in crude exports through its Red Sea terminals, but has yet to stabilize flows at its target level for the route.

title: Data Center Firm Csquare Files Confidentially for US IPO
summary: Csquare filed confidentially for a US initial public offering, a signal that more data center companies are lining up to sell shares for the first time over the coming year.

title: Intel to Hold Fixed-Income Calls Following Blowout Sales Outlook
summary: Intel Corp. is set to hold fixed-income investor calls Friday after delivering a sales forecast that shattered Wall Street expectations.

title: Yara Raises Deliveries as Iran War Jolts Fertilizer Market
summary: Fertilizer giant Yara International ASA boosted shipments in the first quarter as the Iran war effectively halted transit through the Strait of Hormuz, hurting global trade of crop nutrients and pushing up prices.

title: Fund at Center of Avis Squeeze Added to Stake Before Rout
summary: Avis Budget Group Inc.’s second-biggest shareholder Pentwater Capital Management LP boosted its stake in the car rental company just before the stock’s nearly 70% rout.

title: Gold Rises as Pakistan Expects Second Round of US-Iran Talks
summary: Gold edged higher as officials in Pakistan said a second round of US-Iran peace talks was expected in Islamabad, without saying when the negotiations would take place.

title: Collapsing Volatility Turbocharges Returns in Carry Trades
summary: DoubleLine Capital and Van Eck Associates Corp. are among investors seeing renewed appeal in a currency strategy that’s revving up as the Middle East ceasefire helps steady markets and reignite risk appetite.

title: India Mulls Options on Iran Port Stake Before Sanctions Kick In
summary: India is considering various options regarding its stake in the Chabahar port in Iran, including temporarily divesting from it, ahead of the expiry of a US sanctions waiver in coming days, officials familiar with the matter said.

title: The Shape of a Deal to Come
summary: Warsh needed to be asked about the Treasury takeover of monetary policy.

title: US-Sanctioned Supertanker With Iran Oil Halts Hormuz Transit
summary: A US-sanctioned supertanker laden with Iranian oil appeared to halt its transit through the Strait of Hormuz on Friday, with traffic through the waterway otherwise at a virtual standstill.

title: European Stocks Pare Losses on US-Iran Peace Negotiation Hopes
summary: European stocks pared earlier losses as expectations grew that Iran and the US are about to resume peace negotiations.

title: US Premarket Movers for April 24, 2026
summary: S&P 500 Index futures are up 0.4% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York as traders weigh corporate earnings and the US-Iran standoff.

title: China's DeepSeek Unveils AI Model To Challenge Anthropic, OpenAI | The Pulse 4/24
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests:
Wei Li, BlackRock, Global Chief Investment Strategist; Clemens Fuest, Ifo Institute, President; Clément Therme, International Institute for Iranian Studies, Non-Resident Fellow; Francesca Ghiretti, RAND, Europe China Initiative Director.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ‘Bubble’ Signal Flagged by BofA as Tech Stocks, Volatility Soar
summary: A record-smashing rally in technology megacaps has rekindled bubble warnings at Bank of America Corp.

title: Funders Commit $1.3 Billion to Zambia Critical-Minerals Rail
summary: The Africa Finance Corp. and the African Development Bank committed $500 million each to a railway that will connect Zambia’s copper mines to global markets through the Angolan port of Lobito.
